content.cn_text: 第四部分。
content.en_text: Section 4.

content.cn_text: 你将听到一个关于澳大利亚近期鳄鱼研究的报告片段。
content.en_text: You will hear part of a presentation on recent Crocodile Research in Australia.

content.cn_text: 首先，你有一些时间来查看问题31到40。
content.en_text: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听并回答问题31到40。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 大家好，我刚从野外考察回来，马丁教授让我
content.en_text: Hello everybody, I've just arrived back from my field trip and Professor Martin has asked

content.cn_text: 今天在我们的研究论坛上向大家介绍我的考察情况。
content.en_text: me to tell you about it today in our research forum.

content.cn_text: 正如许多人已经知道的，我的论文与鳄鱼行为有关。
content.en_text: As many of you know already, my dissertation relates to crocodile behavior.

content.cn_text: 我的专长是咸水鳄，它比淡水鳄大得多。
content.en_text: My specialty is the saltwater crocodile, which is much larger than the fresh water crocodile.

content.cn_text: 我刚从北领地回来，在那里我参与了一项开创性的
content.en_text: I've just come back from the Northern Territory, where I've been part of a groundbreaking

content.cn_text: 鳄鱼移动观察研究。
content.en_text: study observing crocodile movements.

content.cn_text: 这不是第一次追踪鳄鱼的活动，但这项研究之所以独特
content.en_text: It's not the only time crocodile movements have been followed, but this study is unique

content.cn_text: 是因为我们使用了卫星来追踪鳄鱼。
content.en_text: because we tracked crocodiles using a satellite.

content.cn_text: 到目前为止，类似这样的研究只能通过使用
content.en_text: Up until now, studies like this one have only been able to track crocodile movements using

content.cn_text: 无线电来追踪鳄鱼的活动。
content.en_text: radio.

content.cn_text: 这种方法不仅对动物来说压力更大，因为它们必须被捕捉好几次，
content.en_text: Not only was this method more stressful for the animal, which had to be caught several

content.cn_text: 而且由于信号经常丢失，这种方法也不太可靠。
content.en_text: times, but it was also not very reliable as the signal was often lost.

content.cn_text: 那么，你可能想知道我们为什么要选择追踪鳄鱼。
content.en_text: So, you might be wondering why we chose to track crocodiles.

content.cn_text: 鳄鱼袭击人类和牲畜的情况很少见，但一旦发生，政府
content.en_text: Well, crocodile attacks on people and livestock are rare, but when they do happen, the government

content.cn_text: 认为有问题的鳄鱼应该被重新安置，而不是被杀死。
content.en_text: believes that the problem crocodile should be relocated rather than killed.

content.cn_text: 但我们能确定鳄鱼不会简单地回到它原来的地方吗？
content.en_text: But can we be sure that the crocodile won't simply return to the place where it came

content.cn_text: 呢？
content.en_text: from?

content.cn_text: 因此，我们设计了一项研究来尝试回答这些问题。
content.en_text: So we designed a study to try to answer these questions.

content.cn_text: 我们首先想确定鳄鱼在一段时间内可以行进多远。
content.en_text: We wanted to establish, firstly, how far crocodiles can travel over a period of time.

content.cn_text: 我们想知道的第二件事是鳄鱼导航的容易程度如何？
content.en_text: The second thing that we wanted to know was how easy is it for crocodiles to navigate?

content.cn_text: 如果它们被移到另一个地点，它们还能自己找到回家的路吗？
content.en_text: Could they find their own way home again if they were moved to another location?

content.cn_text: 当然，观察鳄鱼的最大挑战是它们是非常隐秘的
content.en_text: Of course, the biggest challenge when observing crocodiles is that they are very secretive

content.cn_text: 动物。
content.en_text: animals.

content.cn_text: 如果可以，它们会远离人类，这使得它们真的很难被追踪。
content.en_text: They stay well away from people if they can, which makes them really difficult to follow.

content.cn_text: 现在我将给大家介绍一些关于这项研究设计的信息。
content.en_text: I'll give you some information about the design of the study now.

content.cn_text: 我们的研究中有三只鳄鱼，都来自北领地，长度在
content.en_text: There were three crocodiles in our study, all from the Northern Territory, and measuring

content.cn_text: 三到四米半之间。
content.en_text: between three and four and a half meters in length.

content.cn_text: 鳄鱼被捕获、测量和标记，然后由直升机带到新的
content.en_text: The crocodiles were captured, measured and tagged, and then taken by helicopter to new

content.cn_text: 距离发现地点最远400公里的地方。
content.en_text: locations up to 400 kilometers away from where they were found.

content.cn_text: 然后我们就能利用追踪数据来监测它们的活动。
content.en_text: We were then able to use the tracking data to monitor their movements.

content.cn_text: 我们使用了一个专门设计的发射器，将其安装在每只动物的头部。
content.en_text: We used a specially designed transmitter, which we attached to each animal's head.

content.cn_text: 发射器收集数据并反馈给我们。
content.en_text: The transmitter collected the data and related back to us.

content.cn_text: 我们研究的结果相当惊人。
content.en_text: The results of our study were quite astounding.

content.cn_text: 它们被释放后，每只鳄鱼都在释放地点停留了几周，每天
content.en_text: After they were released, each crocodile remained in the release site for some weeks, making

content.cn_text: 在该地点进行随机移动。
content.en_text: random movements around the site each day.

content.cn_text: 但随后，它们都游回了家，走的都是最直接的路线。
content.en_text: But then, they each swam home, traveling by the most direct route.

content.cn_text: 这些鳄鱼展现出非凡的导航能力和耐力，完成了如此漫长的旅程。
content.en_text: These crocodiles showed remarkable navigational skills and stamina to swim such long voyages.

content.cn_text: 那么，我们从中得出了什么结论呢？
content.en_text: So, what conclusions did we draw from this?

content.cn_text: 嗯，我们证明了鳄鱼显然有能力进行长距离游泳来寻找它们的
content.en_text: Well, we proved that crocodiles are clearly capable of making marathon swims to find their

content.cn_text: 家。
content.en_text: home.

content.cn_text: 这意味着现行政策应该被重新考虑。
content.en_text: This meant that the current policy should be reconsidered.

content.cn_text: 你可能想知道鳄鱼是如何回家的。
content.en_text: You might be wondering how the crocodiles managed to get home.

content.cn_text: 我们得出结论，它们使用了诸如视觉和嗅觉等因素，以及它们与
content.en_text: We concluded that they used factors such as sight and smell, as well as their position

content.cn_text: 太阳的位置来指明方向。
content.en_text: to the sun to point their way.

content.cn_text: 鲜为人知的是，鳄鱼与鸟类的关系比它们与任何其他
content.en_text: It's a little known fact that crocodiles are more closely related to birds than they

content.cn_text: 爬行动物更近。
content.en_text: are to any other reptile.

content.cn_text: 因此，我们认为它们可能使用相同的系统进行长途旅行。
content.en_text: So, we believe it's possible that they use the same system to negotiate long journeys.

content.cn_text: 现在研究已经完成，第四部分到此结束。
content.en_text: Now that the study has been completed, that is the end of section 4.

content.cn_text: 你现在有半分钟时间检查你的答案。
content.en_text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.

groups.desc:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.title: Questions 31-40.

items.title: Significance of study: unique because it was the first time a 31. __________ had been used to track crocodiles
stressful method, also unreliable due to loss of the 33. __________
crocodiles are difficult to observe because they avoid 35. __________
method: crocodiles were measured and tagged then relocated using a 36. __________
monitoring: scientists got data from a transmitter located on the crocodile’s 37. __________
Then took the most 38. __________ way home
their own senses and the position of the 39. __________

groups.source_collect:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
Crocodile Study
Background
• Type of crocodile studied: crocodiles found in salt water
• Location of study: Northern Territory, Australia
• Significance of study: unique because it was the first time a $31$ had been used to track crocodiles
Previous studies
• done using $32$
• stressful method, also unreliable due to loss of the $33$
Reasons for study
• the management of crocodiles by the government may not be effective
Research questions
• How far can crocodiles travel?
• How easily can crocodiles $34$?
Challenge
• crocodiles are difficult to observe because they avoid $35$
Details of study
• number of crocodiles involved: three
• method: crocodiles were measured and tagged then relocated using a $36$
• monitoring: scientists got data from a transmitter located on the crocodile’s $37$
Results of study
• Each crocodile first stayed at the site for a few weeks, moving in a random way
• Then took the most $38$ way home
Conclusions
• A change in policy is needed
• When travelling long distances, crocodiles may use
• their own senses and the position of the $39$
• a similar system to $40$
